title: gilia cana subsp. bernardina
intro: Gilia cana subsp. bernardina, also known as San Miguel Island gilia, is a plant species that belongs to the polemonium family and is native to the Channel Islands of California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gilia cana subsp. bernardina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and aid in the treatment of various infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote relaxation, ease coughs, and support the immune system.
